title: Values, Expressions, Names, and Types · Mastery Check
summary: Build a transparent unit and budget calculator whose intermediate values can be inspected and tested. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for an inspectable unit and budget calculator. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish values, objects, names, assignment, equality, and identity in Python execution.
Use integers, floating-point values, decimal-sensitive decisions, arithmetic precedence, and division operators accurately.
Use Boolean values and None as distinct domain states instead of ambiguous numeric or text sentinels.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
